title: A royalty collection and payment system
abstract: A royalty collection and payment system comprising a central computer server operating at least one computer software application to allow at least one party to enter information relating to a creative work and an agreed royalty division payable to one or more stakeholders for use or sale of the creative work or a copy thereof, generation of a unique identification indicium based on the information for inclusion at use or sale of the work or the copy, at least one scanner device in communication with the central computer server to scan the unique identification indicium and the universal product code at a point of sale or use of the work or copy to enable transmission to the central computer server of price information relating to the use or sale of the work or the copy via the universal product code and identify and pay the agreed royalty division.
claims:
Claims 1. A royalty collection and payment system comprising a central computer server with memory and at least one processor, the central computer server operating at least one computer software application to cause generation and display on a remote device of at least one interface into which at least one party enters information relating to a creative work and an agreed royalty division to one or more stakeholders for use or sale of the creative work or a copy thereof, the central server causing generation of a unique identification indicium based on the information for inclusion at use or sale of the work or the copy together with a universal product code, at least one scanner device in communication with the central computer server, the at least one scanner device provided to scan the unique identification indicium and the universal product code at a point of sale or use of the work or copy to enable transmission to the central computer server of price information relating to the use or sale of the work or the copy via the universal product code and identify the agreed royalty division using the unique identification indicium, the central server calculating an amount to be paid to the one or more stakeholders based on the price information and the agreed royalty division and causing payment of a calculated royalty amount to the one or more stakeholders. 2. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 1 wherein the central computer server causes delivery of the unique identification indicium for inclusion at use or sale of the work or the copy. 3. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 1 or claim 2 further comprising access to a collected monies subsystem configured to collect a total amount of royalties owed due to the use or sale of the work or the copy based on the price information. 4. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the unique identification indicium contains a coded visual representation of the information about the agreed royalty division between the one or more stakeholders to be read by the at least one scanner device and decoded using a software application. 

A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 4 wherein the unique identification indicium is or includes a QR code or similar. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein payment details for each of the one or more interested persons are contained in the unique identification indicium. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the central computer system operates at least one computer software program accessed directly or indirectly by the at least one user providing information. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 7 wherein the at least one user providing information enters information into a web-based form or a web hosted interface such that the information is provided to the at least one computer software program operating on the central computer server. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 7 or claim 8 wherein the at least one user providing information provides the information using a secondary software application configured to communicate with the at least one computer software program operating on the central computer system. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the at least one user providing information enters the information as a royalty division proposal and the information forms part of an agreed royalty division only once agreed by the one or more stakeholders. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 10 wherein the software application is configured to facilitate the collection and recording of the agreed royalty division of the one or more stakeholders. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the central computer server is configured to maintain a unique party record for each interested party. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 12 wherein the unique party record will include bank details to allow for payment of the interested party. 14. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the central computer server is configured to maintain a unique work record for each work. 15. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 14 wherein the unique work record for each work will include a royalty division relating to the work. 16. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 14 or claim 15 wherein the unique work record for each work is configured to allow definition of one or more royalty payment conditions as a part of the agreed royalty division. 17. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 16 wherein any one or more royalty payment conditions are stored in the unique work record for the work and managed by the at least one computer software application operating on the central computer server. 18. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the agreed royalty division can be applied to the price information in order to calculate the total royalty amount and the royalty payment amounts for each of the one or more stakeholders. 19. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the unique identification indicium is an electronically produced visual representation or label which can be applied or displayed at the use or sale of the creative work or a copy thereof. 0. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein a first scanner device is provided to read the unique identification indicium and a second scanner device is provided to read the universal product code or similar. 1. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the at least one scanner device is software implemented to read information directly from the unique identification indicium and/or unique product code or similar. 2. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the unique identification indicium and/or unique product code 

 22 or similar are embedded in an electronic interface such that when a customer actuates electronic delivery of a work or copy, the at least one scanner device reads the unique identification indicium and/or unique product code or similar and actions collection of any royalty to be paid. 23. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein collection of the amount to be paid is effected immediately/in real time relative to the use or sale of the work or copy. 24. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in claim 23 wherein the amount to be paid is available for immediate division and payment to the one or more stakeholders according to the agreed royalty division. 25. A royalty collection and payment system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the unique identification indicium is maintained separately to the universal product code or similar.
